app.title: Fire Tools
app.tagline: Rocket fuel for your financial planning
app.disclaimer: Fire Tools - Disclaimer: This is for educational purposes only. Market data is provided as an indication only and may be delayed or inaccurate. Consult with a financial advisor for professional advice.

home.aboutBody: FIRE Tools is designed to help you plan and achieve Financial Independence Retire Early. Our suite of calculators and tools provides data-driven insights to make informed decisions about your financial future.
home.disclaimerBody: These tools are for educational and planning purposes only. Always consult with a qualified financial advisor or do your own research before making investment decisions.
home.securityWarningBody: This application is deployed on GitHub Pages, which does not support server-side data storage or HTTP-only cookies. While data is encrypted using AES, it is still vulnerable to XSS attacks and other client-side exploits that could exfiltrate your financial data.
home.securityRecommendBody: We strongly recommend deploying this application locally on your own machine. Local deployment provides better isolation and reduces attack surface.

assetAllocation.title: Asset Allocation Manager
assetAllocation.body: Manage your investment portfolio with intelligent asset allocation tools, rebalancing strategies, and DCA helper functionality.

cashflow.title: Cashflow Tracker
cashflow.body: Track your income and expenses, set budgets per category, and gain insights into your spending patterns with the 50/30/20 budgeting rule.

netWorth.title: Net Worth Tracker
netWorth.body: Track your financial operations and net worth on a monthly basis. Monitor assets, cash, pensions, and progress toward FIRE.

fireCalculator.title: FIRE Calculator
fireCalculator.body: Calculate your path to financial independence with comprehensive projections and detailed analysis of your retirement timeline.

monteCarlo.title: Monte Carlo Simulations
monteCarlo.body: Run thousands of simulations with randomized market returns to assess the probability of reaching FIRE and account for market volatility.

quiz.title: FIRE Persona Quiz
quiz.body: Discover your FIRE persona and get personalized recommendations based on your financial situation, goals, and risk tolerance.

settings.languageHelp: Choose the language for the user interface. This does not affect your currency.
settings.accountNameHelp: This name will be displayed throughout the app
settings.aiCategorizationHelp: Plug in any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — cloud (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, OpenRouter, Together…) or a self-hosted open-source model via Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, etc. For fully local categorization, point Base URL at e.g. http://localhost:11434/v1, leave the API key as any non-empty string, and use a model you've already pulled (llama3.1:8b, qwen2.5, …).
settings.aiCategorizationStoredHelp: Stored encrypted with your other settings. Only used when you tick “Use AI categorization” in the PDF import dialog.
settings.assetPricesDescription: Current market prices for your portfolio assets. Prices may be delayed.
settings.categoriesDescription: Manage your expense categories. Create custom categories or customize the built-in ones.

settings.dateFormatHelp: How dates are displayed throughout the app
settings.decimalPlacesHelp: Number of decimal places shown for values below 1,000. Values at or above 1,000 show no decimals.

settings.demoDataHelp: Load sample data to explore the application
settings.enableNativeNotificationsHelp: Show macOS / Windows / Linux desktop alerts in addition to in-app ones. Browser users will be asked to grant permission.
settings.exportAllDataHelp: Export all your data in a single file or as separate files
settings.fallbackRatesDescription: These rates are used when the live exchange rate API is unavailable. All values convert to {{currency}} (the default currency).
settings.fetchMarketDataPrompt: Click the button above to fetch current exchange rates and asset prices. Your data stays on your device — only the public market data endpoints are contacted.
settings.fireAssetClassesHelp: Toggle which asset classes are counted in your FIRE portfolio value
settings.fireMilestoneAlertsHelp: Get notified when you reach FIRE milestones (25%, 50%, 75%, 100%)
settings.fireQuestionnaireHelp: Retake the questionnaire to update your FIRE persona and recommendations
settings.guidedTourHelp: Restart the guided tour to learn about Fire Tools features
settings.importAllDataHelp: Import all data from a single JSON file. Data will be converted to your current currency.
settings.importIndividualFilesHelp: Import data from individual CSV/JSON files for each tool.
settings.marketDataDisclaimer: Market data is fetched from free public APIs (Yahoo Finance, exchangerate.host). Values may be delayed, incomplete, or inaccurate, and are not financial advice. Verify critical numbers from authoritative sources before making decisions.

messages.defaultCurrencyChanged: Default currency changed to {{currency}}! All values converted.
messages.ratePositive: Rate must be a positive number
messages.testNotificationsCreated: {{count}} test notifications created! Check the notification bell.

settings.newMonthRemindersHelp: Remind to update financial data at the start of each month
settings.pdfImportHelp: Parse receipts, invoices, bank statements, and payslips locally. No network calls unless you opt into AI categorization — which itself can be pointed at a self-hosted open-source model so nothing leaves your machine.
settings.portfolioBreakdownHelp: Enable the Portfolio Breakdown page in navigation
settings.primaryResidenceHelp: Controls whether your primary residence counts toward your FIRE target
settings.privacyModeHelp: Blur sensitive financial data on screen for privacy
settings.reportBugHelp: Report bugs, UI issues, or calculation errors

settings.title: Settings

tooltips.loggingPii: Controls whether diagnostic logs include sensitive financial details. Logs never leave your device unless you export them.
tooltips.maxLogFileSize: Maximum on-disk log file size before older entries are rotated out. Logs live next to your database.

settings.uxUiSuggestionHelp: Suggest improvements to the user interface or experience

backend.description: Choose where the app stores and reads your data. By default Fire Tools runs an embedded backend inside the desktop app, with local-only SQLite storage. Advanced users can point the app at a separately-running backend instance.
backend.embeddedOnlyDesktop: Embedded backend is only available in the desktop app.
backend.customUrlHelp: Enter the origin (scheme + host + port) of a running Fire Tools backend. The app will append /api/v1 automatically.

advanced.description: Inspect or smoke-test the embedded backend's REST API. Useful for debugging and verifying connectivity.
advanced.pathHelp: Path is appended to the backend's /api/v1 base URL.
advanced.noBackend: No backend is configured. Set one in the Backend section above.
advanced.invalidJson: Request body is not valid JSON

settings.loggingPiiHelp: When off (default), diagnostic logs strip out financial details (tickers, amounts, account names). When on, full context is kept locally to help debug issues.
settings.loggingPiiWarning: Detailed logs may contain financial data. Only share exported logs with people you trust.
settings.maxLogFileSizeHelp: Caps the on-disk log file (1–500 MB, default 50). When exceeded, the oldest entries roll into a single backup file. Only applies to the desktop app.

updater.description: Configure auto-updates and manage local backups.
updater.desktopOnly: Auto-update is only available in the desktop app.
updater.autoCheckHelp: On launch and periodically while the app is open.
updater.autoDownloadHelp: When off, you choose when to download a new version.
updater.notifyOnlyHelp: Show a notification when an update exists but never download or install.
updater.keepBackupsHelp: Older backups are pruned. At least 1 is always kept.
updater.confirmRestore: Restore this backup? Your current data will be replaced. A safety snapshot will be taken first.

feedback.disabledDev: Auto-update is disabled in development builds.
feedback.missingDep: Auto-update support is missing in this build.
feedback.restored: Backup restored. Restart the app to apply.

fireCalculator.title: FIRE Calculator Inputs

labels.yearsOfExpenses: Years of Expenses Needed for FIRE

fireCalculator.deflationWarning: A positive value indicates deflation. Long periods of deflation are historically rare - consider this carefully in your projections.

standard.label: Standard FIRE
standard.howItWorks:
Formula: target = fireAnnualExpenses × yearsOfExpenses (e.g. 25× at a 4% safe withdrawal rate).

Post-FIRE: the portfolio funds your full target expenses. If "stop working at FIRE" is enabled, labor income stops; otherwise contributions continue.

Best for: anyone using the Trinity-study definition of FIRE.

lean.label: Lean FIRE
lean.howItWorks:
Formula: target = fireAnnualExpenses × leanExpenseMultiplier × yearsOfExpenses (default multiplier 0.7).

Post-FIRE: the same as Standard — only the target is scaled down. Real-world expenses still use fireAnnualExpenses, so leave a buffer or lower your fireAnnualExpenses to truly live lean.

Best for: low cost-of-living locations, minimalists, anyone who wants to reach independence years earlier.

fat.label: Fat FIRE
fat.howItWorks:
Formula: target = fireAnnualExpenses × fatExpenseMultiplier × yearsOfExpenses (default multiplier 2.0).

Post-FIRE: same projection logic as Standard, but with a much larger target. Use this when your expected lifestyle in retirement is higher than today.

Best for: high-income earners who want a luxury retirement, or to bake in a generous safety margin.

barista.label: Barista FIRE
barista.howItWorks:
Formula: target = max(0, fireAnnualExpenses − baristaAnnualIncome) × yearsOfExpenses.

Post-FIRE: labor income drops to baristaAnnualIncome and stops growing. The portfolio only has to fund the gap between your expenses and that part-time income, so the nest egg can be much smaller than Standard FIRE.

Best for: people who enjoy working but want flexibility, healthcare coverage, or to retire from a high-stress career earlier.

coast.label: Coast FIRE
coast.howItWorks:
Formula: target = standardTarget / (1 + expectedReturn)^(coastTargetAge − currentAge).

Post-FIRE: you keep your job and salary, but stop new contributions. Compounding alone grows the portfolio to the full Standard FIRE target by your chosen target age.

Best for: anyone who front-loads savings in their 20s/30s and then wants the freedom to switch to a lower-paying or more meaningful career.

welcome.title: Welcome to Fire Tools!
welcome.paragraph1Start: Welcome! Fire Tools is a comprehensive suite of financial planning tools designed to help you achieve
welcome.paragraph2: This quick tour will show you the main features and how to get the most out of the application. We'll also load some demo data so you can see everything in action.
welcome.privacyNote: All your data stays on your device - we never send it anywhere.

together.title: Tools Work Together
together.strong: The real power comes from using FIRE tools together, but each tool can also work independently!
together.assetValues: Asset values flow into FIRE Calculator
together.netWorthSync: Net Worth syncs with Asset Allocation

initialSavings.title: Initial Savings
initialSavings.description: Enter your current savings or portfolio value. This is your starting point for FIRE calculations.

annualIncome.title: Annual Income
annualIncome.description: Enter your annual net labor income. This determines how much you can save each year toward FIRE.

annualExpenses.title: Annual Expenses
annualExpenses.description: Set your current annual expenses. Lower expenses mean a faster path to FIRE!

withdrawalRate.title: Withdrawal Rate
withdrawalRate.description: The withdrawal rate (typically 3-4%) determines your FIRE target. A 4% rate means you need 25x your annual expenses.

integrationOptions.title: Integration Options
integrationOptions.description: These options connect tools together: sync portfolio values from Asset Allocation, or use actual income/expenses from Cashflow Tracker for more accurate calculations.

fireMetrics.title: FIRE Metrics
fireMetrics.description: See your FIRE target, years to FIRE, and projected portfolio value. These are your key milestones!

growthCharts.title: Growth Charts
growthCharts.description: Visualize your journey! The Net Worth Growth chart shows your portfolio over time, and Income vs Expenses shows your cash flow.

yourAssets.title: Your Assets
yourAssets.description: This table shows your portfolio holdings. You can see each asset's current value, target allocation, and recommended action (BUY/SELL/HOLD).

addNewAssets.title: Add New Assets
addNewAssets.description: Click the "Add Asset" button to add a new holding to your portfolio. Go ahead, click it now!

assetClassSelection.title: Asset Class Selection
assetClassSelection.description: First, choose the asset class (Stocks, Bonds, Cash, Crypto, or Real Estate). This determines how the asset is categorized in your portfolio.

assetDetails.title: 🏷️ Asset Details
assetDetails.description: Enter the asset name, ticker symbol (e.g., AAPL, VTI), number of shares you own, and the price per share. The total value is calculated automatically.

targetAllocation.title: Target Allocation
targetAllocation.description: Set your target allocation mode: "%" for percentage of the asset class, "SET" for a fixed amount, or "OFF" to exclude from rebalancing.

targetAllocations.title: Target Allocations
targetAllocations.description: Set your target percentages for each asset class. The tool calculates what to buy or sell to reach your targets.

dcaHelper.title: 💹 DCA Helper
dcaHelper.description: The DCA (Dollar Cost Averaging) Helper calculates how to split a lump sum investment. Click the "DCA Helper" button to see how it works!

enterInvestmentAmount.title: Enter Investment Amount
enterInvestmentAmount.description: Enter the amount you want to invest. The DCA Helper will calculate how to split it across your assets according to your target allocations.

investmentBreakdown.title: Investment Breakdown
investmentBreakdown.description: After calculating, you'll see exactly how much to invest in each asset and how many shares to buy. Current prices are fetched from Yahoo Finance.

confirm.title: ✅ Confirm
confirm.description: After making your investments, you can confirm the actual shares purchased to track deviations from the suggested amounts.

addIncome.title: Add Income
addIncome.description: Track your income and expenses here. Click "Add Income" to see how to add income!

dateAmount.title: 📅 Date & Amount
dateAmount.description: Select the date when you received the income and enter the amount. This helps track your income over time.

incomeSource.title: Income Source
incomeSource.description: Choose the income source: Salary, Bonus, Dividend, Freelance, Investment, Rental, or Other. This categorizes your income for better analytics.

addExpense.title: 💸 Add Expense
addExpense.description: Now click "Add Expense" to see how to record your spending.

expenseDetails.title: 📅 Expense Details
expenseDetails.description: Enter the date, amount, and a description of the expense. Be specific to help you track spending patterns.

categoryType.title: 🏷️ Category & Type
categoryType.description: Select a category (Housing, Food, Transport, etc.) and classify as Need or Want. This powers the 50/30/20 budget analysis.

budgetRule.title: 50/30/20 Budget Rule
budgetRule.description: See how your spending compares to the 50/30/20 rule: 50% for needs (housing, food), 30% for wants (entertainment), and 20% for savings.

setBudgets.title: Set Budgets
setBudgets.description: Click the Budgets tab to set monthly spending limits for each category. Track your progress with visual indicators.

budgetCategories.title: Budget Categories
budgetCategories.description: Here you can set monthly budget limits for each expense category. The progress bars show how much you've spent vs. your budget. Edit the amounts to customize your spending goals.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Click the Analytics tab to see spending trends, category breakdowns, and monthly comparisons to help you understand your financial habits.

spendingInsights.title: Spending Insights
spendingInsights.description: View detailed charts and analytics: spending trends over time, category breakdowns, and monthly comparisons. Use the view selector to switch between monthly, quarterly, and yearly perspectives.

logAssets.title: Log Assets
logAssets.description: Just like in Asset Allocation, you can add assets here to track your historical net worth. Click "Log Asset" to add stocks, bonds, crypto, real estate, and other holdings with their current values.

logOperations.title: Log Operations
logOperations.description: Click "Log Operation" to track financial operations like dividends, purchases, and sales.

operationDetails.title: 📅 Operation Details
operationDetails.description: Select the date and operation type: Dividend, Purchase, Sale, Deposit, Withdrawal, Interest, or Tax.

amountDescription.title: Amount & Description
amountDescription.description: Enter the amount and a description. Income operations (dividends, interest) increase your net worth; expenses (taxes) decrease it.

historicalChart.title: Historical Chart
historicalChart.description: Watch your wealth grow over time! The chart shows your net worth progression with monthly variations and forecasted growth.

assetAllocationSync.title: Asset Allocation Sync
assetAllocationSync.description: Enable sync to automatically keep your asset data consistent between Net Worth Tracker and Asset Allocation Manager.

baseData.title: Simulation Base Data
baseData.description: This section shows the FIRE Calculator data used for simulations: your portfolio value, FIRE target, income, savings rate, and expected returns. These values come from your FIRE Calculator inputs.

parameters.title: Simulation Parameters
parameters.description: Adjust the simulation settings: number of simulations (1000-100000), stock/bond volatility (higher = more uncertain), and black swan event probability. These affect how conservative or optimistic your projections are.

runSimulation.title: ▶️ Run Simulation
runSimulation.description: Click the "Run Simulations" button to run the Monte Carlo analysis. This will generate thousands of scenarios based on your parameters.

results.title: Results & Success Rate
results.description: See your success probability, median outcome, and the range of possible results. Green means high probability of success!

logs.title: Simulation Logs
logs.description: After running simulations, you can view detailed logs showing the yearly breakdown of each simulation, including returns, expenses (adjusted for inflation), and portfolio values. Export the logs as CSV or JSON for further analysis.

tables.calculatedValueTitle: Current value is calculated from shares × price per share

charts.startTrackingNetWorth: Start tracking your net worth to see the historical chart.
charts.netWorthChartAriaLabel: Net worth growth chart over time
charts.incomeExpensesChartAriaLabel: Income and expenses comparison chart over time
charts.noMonthlyData: No monthly data to display. Add transactions to see the comparison.
charts.noExpenseData: No expense data to display. Add some expenses to see the breakdown.
charts.noTrendData: No trend data to display. Add transactions across multiple months to see trends.

dataManagement.title: Data Management
dataManagement.loadDemoAriaLabel: Load demo expense data for the current year
dataManagement.resetAriaLabel: Reset all calculator data to defaults

demo.message: Read-only demo with sample data — imports and resets are disabled. Download the app to save your own data.
demo.disabledAction: Disabled in demo. Download the app to import your data.

blocks.html: Welcome to Fire Tools (the "Application"). This Privacy Policy explains how we handle your personal data when you use our financial planning tools. Your privacy is fundamental to us, and this policy outlines our commitment to protecting your personal information.

Fire Tools is a financial planning application designed with privacy as a core principle. All your data is stored locally on your device and is never transmitted to external servers without your explicit consent.
The data controller responsible for processing your personal data is:

items: Application Name: Fire Tools
Repository: github.com/mbianchidev/fire-tools
Contact: security@mb-consulting.dev

blocks.html: Fire Tools collects only the data you voluntarily provide when using the calculator and asset allocation features. This includes:

items: Initial savings amount
Annual income and expenses
Investment expectations and risk tolerance
Retirement age and FIRE goals
Asset allocation preferences
Portfolio holdings and performance data
Currency settings
Display preferences
Privacy and cookie consent choices
Personal identification information (name, email, address)
Authentication credentials
Browsing history outside the application
Device fingerprints or tracking identifiers
Location data
Contact information

blocks.html: Under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), our legal basis for processing your data is:
All data you input is used solely for:

items: Financial Calculations: Running FIRE projections, Monte Carlo simulations, and asset allocation analysis
Local Storage: Maintaining your data between sessions on your device
Application Functionality: Providing the features and tools you've requested

blocks.html: We do not:

items: Share your data with third parties
Sell your data to anyone
Use your data for advertising or marketing
Profile you for any purpose
Transfer your data internationally

blocks.prefix: For details about our use of cookies, please see our

blocks.html: We do NOT share your data with anyone. Since your data never leaves your device, there is no data to share. We do not:

items: Sell your personal data
Share data with service providers (we have none)
Send data to analytics companies
Transmit data to advertising networks
Share data with business partners
Disclose data to legal authorities (we don't have access to it)

blocks.html: Not applicable. Your data remains on your device and is never transferred anywhere, domestically or internationally.

rows: Stored in cookies for 365 days, or until you clear your browser data
Stored in cookies for 365 days, or until you clear your browser data
Stored in cookies for 365 days, or until you clear your browser data

blocks.html: You have complete control over your data. You can delete all stored data at any time using the "Reset All Data" option in the Settings page or by clearing your browser cookies.
Under GDPR and other privacy laws, you have the following rights:

items: Right to Access: All your data is accessible through the Fire Tools interface
Right to Rectification: You can edit your data at any time through the calculator inputs
Right to Erasure: Use the "Reset All Data" button or clear your browser cookies
Right to Restrict Processing: Stop using the application or delete your data
Right to Data Portability: Export your data as CSV files at any time
Right to Object: You can stop using the application at any time
Right to Withdraw Consent: Delete your data or stop using the application

blocks.html: Since all data is stored locally on your device, you have direct control over your data without needing to contact us.
We implement robust security measures to protect your data:

items: AES-256 Encryption: All sensitive financial data is encrypted before storage
HTTPS/TLS: The application is served over secure HTTPS connections
Secure Cookies: Cookies use SameSite=Strict and Secure flags
No Server Storage: Your data never reaches our servers (we don't have any)
No Network Transmission: Data is never sent over the network
Open Source: Full transparency - audit our code on GitHub

blocks.html: Your Responsibility: Keep your device secure. Anyone with access to your device and browser can view your Fire Tools data.
Fire Tools is intended for adults planning their financial future. We do not knowingly collect data from children under 16. Since we don't collect any data, this is inherently protected.
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or legal requirements. When we make changes, we will update the "Last Updated" date at the top of this policy.
Material changes will be announced through our GitHub repository. Continued use of Fire Tools after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
For questions, concerns, or requests related to this Privacy Policy:

items: Email: security@mb-consulting.dev
GitHub: github.com/mbianchidev/fire-tools

blocks.html: This Privacy Policy is governed by applicable data protection laws including GDPR (EU), CCPA (California), and other relevant privacy regulations. Since we don't collect or store data, compliance is inherently maintained.
This Cookie Policy explains how Fire Tools uses cookies and similar tracking technologies. We are committed to transparency about our data practices and your privacy.
Our approach is simple: We only use cookies that are essential for the application to function. We do not use any tracking, analytics, advertising, or third-party cookies.
Cookies are small text files stored on your device by your web browser when you visit a website. They allow the website to remember your preferences and provide a better user experience.
Cookies can be classified by:

items: Duration: Session cookies (deleted when you close the browser) or persistent cookies (remain for a set period)
Source: First-party cookies (set by the website you visit) or third-party cookies (set by external services)
Purpose: Essential, functional, analytics, or advertising cookies

blocks.html: Fire Tools uses ONLY first-party, essential cookies. These cookies are necessary for the application to function and cannot be disabled without affecting core functionality.

rows: Stores your calculator inputs (savings, expenses, returns, etc.) for persistence between sessions
Stores your asset allocation data for portfolio tracking
Stores your target asset allocation percentages for rebalancing
Stores your application preferences (currency, display settings, etc.)

blocks.html: Fire Tools does not use any of the following types of cookies:

blocks.title: ✅ No Tracking, No Analytics, No Advertising
blocks.html: Your privacy is paramount. We have made a conscious decision to not implement any non-essential cookies.

items: Analytics Cookies: No Google Analytics, Mixpanel, or any other analytics service
Advertising Cookies: No advertising networks or tracking pixels
Social Media Cookies: No Facebook, Twitter, or other social media tracking
Third-Party Cookies: No cookies from external services or partners
Performance Cookies: No performance monitoring or heatmaps
Marketing Cookies: No remarketing or behavioral targeting

blocks.html: All cookies used by Fire Tools are protected by industry-standard security measures:

items: AES-256 Encryption: Sensitive financial data is encrypted before being stored in cookies
SameSite=Strict: Cookies are restricted to first-party context only, preventing CSRF attacks
Secure Flag: Cookies are only transmitted over HTTPS connections in production
HttpOnly: Not applicable as we need JavaScript access for the calculator functionality

blocks.html: You have several options to manage cookies:
You can control cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to:

items: View what cookies are stored
Delete existing cookies
Block all cookies or specific types
Be notified before cookies are set

blocks.html: For more information on managing cookies in your browser:

items: Google Chrome
Mozilla Firefox
Apple Safari
Microsoft Edge

blocks.html: Within Fire Tools, you can:

items: Reset All Data: Use the "Reset All Data" button in Settings to clear all stored data
Privacy Mode: Toggle privacy mode to hide sensitive financial information
Export Data: Download your data as CSV files before clearing cookies

blocks.html: Since all our cookies are essential, disabling them will affect the application:

items: Your calculator inputs will not be saved between sessions
Your asset allocation data will be lost when you close the browser
Your preferences will need to be set each time
Some features may not work correctly

blocks.html: If you prefer not to use cookies, you can still use Fire Tools but will need to re-enter your data each session.

In addition to cookies, Fire Tools may use the browser's localStorage API for certain functionality. localStorage is similar to cookies but offers more storage space and is not sent with HTTP requests.
Like our cookies, localStorage is used only for essential functionality:

items: Storing larger datasets locally
Caching application state
Maintaining user preferences

blocks.html: You can clear localStorage through your browser's developer tools or by using the application's reset functionality.
Our cookie usage complies with major privacy regulations:

items: GDPR (EU): Compliant - essential cookies only, no consent required for strictly necessary cookies
CCPA (California): Compliant - no sale of personal information
ePrivacy Directive (EU): Compliant - essential cookies are exempt from consent requirements
LGPD (Brazil): Compliant - minimal data processing
PIPEDA (Canada): Compliant - consent obtained for cookie usage

blocks.html: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our cookie practices or legal requirements. When we make changes, we will:

items: Update the "Last Updated" date at the top of this policy
Notify users through the application's cookie consent banner
Document significant changes in our GitHub repository

blocks.html: We encourage you to review this Cookie Policy periodically.
If you have questions about our use of cookies or this Cookie Policy:

items: Email: security@mb-consulting.dev
GitHub: github.com/mbianchidev/fire-tools
Issues: Report any concerns on our GitHub issue tracker

blocks.prefix: For more information about how we handle your data, please see our
blocks.suffix: . Together, these policies provide a complete picture of our privacy practices.
